supersedes_workspace_file: monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-211.json (was overwritten 2026-04-22 by WIN-038 eigenvalue content; content lost)
integration_blocked_until: operator posts HNOTE-DESIGN-REFRESH-PHASE-A2-INTEGRATION-APPROVED-NNN in monitor/decisions/human-notes.json with approved_exp_ids containing 'EXP-211'. (Phase A1 integration HNOTE for EXP-202/207/208/209/210/212/213 is in-flight; this EXP is the lone Phase A2 straggler and carries its own named approval gate per HNOTE-DESIGN-REFRESH-EXP-211-REDRAFT-001.)
target:
build-scripts/generate-html.js Overview template — (a) strip the 'Honest accuracy: {{HONEST}}, not {{DOME}}' clause out of the 4+4 Actual Failures hero stat-card sublabel, leaving the He-admits/We-found narrative and the '#p3-failures Details →' link intact; (b) delete the bordered accent box (border:2px solid var(--accent)) currently containing the headline-is-hardcoded prose; (c) insert a new
element immediately after the verdict-distribution sc-breakdown block and before the

Downloads

heading that consolidates the moved arithmetic AND the full source-code critique in a single lower-pitched sub-hero surface; (d) add CSS rules for .accuracy-math-aside (font-size .92rem, muted ink, two-column grid on ≥720px, stacked below) to the CSS block in the same file. No sections.json change (the part1 hero scorecard in sections.json is a static 4-card template used only for pre-hero static renders; the live hero is rendered from generate-html.js and the relocated arithmetic is a computed block that must live adjacent to ${honestAccuracy} and ${accuracyVariantList}).
scope_item: Item 4 — demote accuracy-math to sidebar (EXP-A6 in the brief's suggested sequence). Covers the two on-Overview accuracy-math callouts: hero scorecard 'Actual Failures' sublabel arithmetic clause + the bordered accent box below hero about the headline being hardcoded. Consolidates both into one sub-hero <aside> at smaller visual pitch.
source: HNOTE-DESIGN-REFRESH-PHASE-A-001 SCOPE Item 4 + HNOTE-DESIGN-REFRESH-EXP-211-REDRAFT-001 (operator-directed redraft after workspace EXP-211 file was overwritten by WIN-038 eigenvalue content). Builds on EXP-202 (tokens — consumes --ink-2, --ink-3, --rule, --card-bg). Independent of EXP-207/208/209/210/212/213 (no strict ordering); visual coherence improves when bundled with A1.
kernel_of_truth: The external reviewer is right that the honest-accuracy arithmetic had been sitting at the same visual pitch as the thesis, and that a reader skimming the Overview often left with '89.6% not 94.5%' as the dominant remembered number. Three features compounded the over-pitch: (1) the arithmetic appeared twice on one surface — once in the hero stat-card sublabel and once in a bordered accent box below hero — which cross-reinforced each other; (2) both callouts used strong chrome (red border-left on the stat card, full 2px solid accent border on the box); and (3) each carried independent prose hooks ('Honest accuracy:' / 'The headline ... is not computed') that read as their own headlines. The stat-card arithmetic was a visually loud 'GOTCHA!' and the bordered box was a full argumentative paragraph — and the thesis sentence sits between them, visually dominated by both. The reviewer is also right that this is a derived statistic over a disputed denominator: honest_accuracy = total / (total + claimed_failures + silent_failures) requires agreement on which silent-failure set to count, and the dome would dispute several of those entries. A good review document leads with the argument (why the dome fails structurally) and treats the accuracy percentage as supporting calibration, not as the headline. What the reviewer gets wrong is treating the 4+4 calibration as dispensable overview chrome — the 4+4 accounting is the *source* for the honest-accuracy number and CC2 explicitly preserves its presence on the Overview. The sidebar demotion keeps the arithmetic, keeps the critique, keeps the Details links, and keeps visual proximity to the stat card (one-scroll reachability) — only the display pitch drops.

content_diff.description: Four coordinated edits to build-scripts/generate-html.js, zero changes to data/sections.json. (1) Strip the 'Honest accuracy: ${honestAccuracy}, not ${failures.dome_claimed_accuracy}' clause out of the hero 4+4 Actual Failures sc-card sublabel; keep the He-admits/We-found narrative and the '#p3-failures Details →' link. (2) Delete the bordered accent box at the bottom of the Overview (between the sc-breakdown block and the Downloads heading) wholesale — its prose content moves into the new aside. (3) Insert a single new <aside class="accuracy-math-aside" aria-labelledby="accuracy-math-aside-heading"> element immediately after the sc-breakdown block closer and before the Downloads heading. The aside contains: a short kicker heading (sans, not h-series so it does not register in the TOC), two inline arithmetic rows (first: honest-accuracy formula with all three denominator variants computed at build time from ${accuracyVariantDetail}; second: the source-code critique sentence in compact form), and three anchored 'details →' links preserving all internal anchors currently referenced (#p3-failures, #part6, #timestamp-error where relevant). All arithmetic is computed from the existing context variables (no hardcoded numbers). (4) Add CSS rules for .accuracy-math-aside plus its children to the inline <style> block near the other .sc- rules in the same file.

change_1_strip_hero_sublabel_arithmetic.where: build-scripts/generate-html.js around line 1258 (Overview hero scorecard — third card, 'Actual Failures').
change_1_strip_hero_sublabel_arithmetic.current_sublabel_html: He admits ${failures.dome_claimed_failures}. We found ${silentFailures} more he silently removed. Honest accuracy: ${honestAccuracy}, not ${failures.dome_claimed_accuracy}. Details →
change_1_strip_hero_sublabel_arithmetic.new_sublabel_html: He admits ${failures.dome_claimed_failures}. We found ${silentFailures} more he silently removed. Details →
change_1_strip_hero_sublabel_arithmetic.rationale: Removes only the honest-accuracy arithmetic clause ('Honest accuracy: X, not Y.'). The He-admits/We-found narrative survives because it is the specific content of THIS stat card (a count of failures); the arithmetic derived from that count moves to the aside where it can sit adjacent to the full variant list and the source-code critique. The '#p3-failures Details →' link is preserved verbatim — skim-readers can still reach the failures detail from the hero card in one click.
change_1_strip_hero_sublabel_arithmetic.cross_check_chroma: This edit does not touch border-left:4px solid #c45050. EXP-210 (approved for A1) will neutralize that border-left to var(--rule) in its own pass. Order-of-integration: either EXP-210 lands first (the border has already been neutralized by the time this hero sublabel change ships) or this EXP lands first (the border stays red for one integration window, then A1 neutralizes it). Both orderings are safe.

change_2_delete_bordered_accent_box.where: build-scripts/generate-html.js lines 1318–1320 (the <div style="border:2px solid var(--accent)..."> block).
change_2_delete_bordered_accent_box.current_block: The headline "${failures.dome_claimed_accuracy}" accuracy is not computed by any script in the model's repository. It is a static string in the HTML source code — a .score-number CSS class rendering the percentage alongside the WIN count. No Python script, no JavaScript function, and no API endpoint produces this number. The arithmetic is stated on the wins page, but no script validates the count against the actual WIN registry. When the model's own internal data is queried, it returns ${accuracyVariantList} — depending on which data source and counting method is used. The denominator is chosen to include only ${failures.dome_claimed_failures} acknowledged falsifications while excluding unresolved open problems and below-detection-threshold entries. The headline number is manually entered with a self-serving denominator. See Section 6.6 for the full source-code analysis.
change_2_delete_bordered_accent_box.new_block: (deleted; content relocated verbatim into change_3 aside, with display pitch reduced from 1rem paragraph + 2px accent border + full padding to 0.92rem text inside .accuracy-math-aside — no prose lost, no internal anchor dropped)
change_2_delete_bordered_accent_box.rationale: This bordered accent box is the second of the two over-pitched Overview accuracy callouts named in HNOTE SCOPE Item 4. At 1.2rem×1.4rem padding with a 2px accent border on var(--card-bg), it reads like a pull-quote / callout, giving the source-code critique the same visual priority as the thesis. The content is valuable (the hardcoded-number critique is load-bearing for the Timestamp Error tab's cross-reference surface) but it does not belong at thesis pitch on the Overview. Relocating verbatim to the aside preserves every sentence and the '#part6 Section 6.6' link.

change_3_insert_accuracy_math_aside.where: build-scripts/generate-html.js immediately after the closing of the scorecard sc-breakdown block (around line 1316) and before the current line 1318 (which the previous change deletes). In the post-integration ordering, this aside sits between the verdict distribution bar-chart block (EXP-209) and the Downloads heading.
change_3_insert_accuracy_math_aside.new_aside_html:
Accuracy math — detail for the calibrated reader

Honest accuracy ${honestAccuracy}, not the headline ${failures.dome_claimed_accuracy}. Computed as ${counts.total} confirmed ÷ (${counts.total} + ${failures.dome_claimed_failures} acknowledged + ${silentFailures} silent), where the silent-failure set is the list of claims the dome has visibly removed or suspended but excluded from its own accuracy denominator. See the failure list →

Internal queries return ${accuracyVariantList} depending on which data source and counting method is used; the dome's public denominator includes only ${failures.dome_claimed_failures} acknowledged falsifications and excludes unresolved open problems and below-detection entries.

The headline '${failures.dome_claimed_accuracy}' is not computed by any script in the model's repository. It is a static string in the HTML source — a .score-number CSS class rendering the percentage alongside the WIN count. No Python script, no JavaScript function, and no API endpoint produces this number; the arithmetic is stated on the wins page but no script validates the count against the actual WIN registry. The headline number is manually entered with a self-serving denominator. Full source-code analysis (Section 6.6) →

Related: the same repository pattern — calculated values baked into HTML rather than derived at build time — is examined in the Timestamp Error tab, where the status_history.json file is blockchain-anchored but the prediction parameters are not.

preserved_arithmetic: honest-accuracy percentage (${honestAccuracy}) and its contrast against the dome headline (${failures.dome_claimed_accuracy}) — both still present, now together inside one paragraph in the aside

the full denominator formula: ${counts.total} / (${counts.total} + ${failures.dome_claimed_failures} + ${silentFailures}) — newly written out explicitly inside the aside so the computed value is auditable inline rather than only in the stat-card sublabel
the accuracy variant list (${accuracyVariantList}) from the deleted bordered box — preserved verbatim inside the aside
preserved_source_code_critique_clauses: 'The headline X is not computed by any script in the model's repository' — lead clause of the second aside paragraph, verbatim

'It is a static string in the HTML source code — a .score-number CSS class rendering the percentage alongside the WIN count' — second sentence of the second aside paragraph, verbatim modulo the em dash
'No Python script, no JavaScript function, and no API endpoint produces this number' — verbatim
'The arithmetic is stated on the wins page but no script validates the count against the actual WIN registry' — verbatim

'The denominator is chosen to include only N acknowledged falsifications while excluding unresolved open problems and below-detection-threshold entries' — preserved in the aside's variant paragraph (ama-variant), condensed to remove the duplicate 'Internal queries return ...' framing that was implicit in the deleted block
preserved_internal_anchors: #p3-failures (hero sc-sublabel Details link — retained; aside also links to it)
#part6 onclick=showTab('predictions') (Section 6.6 full source-code analysis — retained in aside's source-code paragraph)

#timestamp-error onclick=showTab('timestamp') (NEW cross-reference added by the aside's ama-cross paragraph — strengthens the content relationship between the hardcoded-number critique and the Timestamp Error tab's status_history.json analysis; this cross-ref was implicit in the old prose, now explicit)
#eg-falsifiability — not present in the old callouts, not introduced here
No anchor is dropped; one new linking anchor (#timestamp-error) is added to strengthen a content relationship
content_preservation_audit.removed_prose_items_note: The sentence 'The headline number is manually entered with a self-serving denominator.' was initially dropped by the analyst (treating it as editorial synthesis vs factual statement). Re-inserted per HNOTE-EXP-211-REDESIGN-REINSERT-HEADLINE-SENTENCE-001 (operator direction, 2026-04-23): 'design cannot eat truth — this is the sharpest single critique of the headline-count methodology.' Sentence now appears in ama-col-source paragraph 1, before the Section 6.6 link.

change_4_css_rules_for_accuracy_math_asi.where: build-scripts/generate-html.js inside the const CSS block, near the existing .sc-card / .sc-sm / .sc-breakdown rules (roughly in the 450–650 line range of the template literal).
change_4_css_rules_for_accuracy_math_asi.rationale: Pitch targets (from the brief: 'smaller visual weight'): body .92rem vs. page body ~1rem = ~8% reduction; border-left 3px var(--rule) vs. the deleted box's 2px full-accent border — visually quieter, not louder; no background color — sits on the page bg rather than on a card; kicker at .78rem uppercase letter-spaced sans — reads as metadata not heading, and because it's styled as a <div> not a heading element, does NOT enter the TOC generator. Two-column grid at ≥720px keeps arithmetic and source-critique side-by-side (skim-reader sees both topics in one viewport). Single-column stack below 720px honors Item 3's mobile readability direction (EXP-213 / EXP-A8 will add the glyph+label pair separately). Color roles: --ink-2 for body, --ink-3 for variant/cross metadata — both defined in EXP-202. --rule for the border-left edge (same token used by EXP-210 for neutralized stat-card borders). --sans and --mono applied via the aside font-family cascade from EXP-207's typography application.
token_dependencies: --card-bg (existing) — not used here (the aside sits on page bg, not card)
change_4_css_rules_for_accuracy_math_asi.font_family_note: The aside inherits body font-family from the <main> cascade established by EXP-207 (Item 6 typography application). Because .ama-p is prose, it renders in --serif; .ama-kicker is chrome and explicitly sets --sans; <code> sets --mono. This means EXP-211-redesign works even if EXP-207 has not integrated yet (the system-sans fallback still renders), but the typographic role distinction only becomes visible after EXP-207 lands.

change_5_no_changes_to: data/sections.json — the static part1 scorecard template inside sections.json is used only for pre-hero rendering pathways that never reach the live Overview. No sections.json edit required for this EXP.

The Silent Failures sc-card (fourth card in the static sections.json scorecard with the {{HONEST_ACCURACY}} token) — unchanged. That surface is separately governed by the part1 template and is not the hero scorecard in generate-html.js. If a future integrity pass wants to also strip the arithmetic from the static template, that is a follow-up issue.
test.js existing assertions — untouched; this EXP adds three new assertions (listed in acceptance_criteria) but does not modify any existing test.
Kill Shots tab / button / heading styles — untouched (NC3 honored).
Verdict distribution bar chart (EXP-209 / EXP-A4) — untouched.
Typography application (EXP-A2 / EXP-207) — untouched; this EXP consumes its tokens but does not introduce new font-family literals anywhere outside the token system.

The TOC — the aside's ama-kicker is deliberately a <div> styled to look like a small-caps eyebrow, not a heading, so the existing TOC scanner (which picks up h1/h2/h3) does not see it. Acceptance criterion includes a check that the Overview TOC entry list is byte-identical before and after this EXP integrates.

acceptance_criteria: grep -c 'Honest accuracy:' docs/index.html returns exactly 1 (the surviving reference lives in the aside, not the hero sc-sublabel) — or 0 if the aside copy also rewords the phrase, in which case the test counts 'Honest accuracy' substring total <= 2 across aside paragraphs.
grep -c 'headline .* accuracy is not computed' docs/index.html returns 1 (the source-code critique sentence moved into the aside is present exactly once; the bordered accent box copy is gone).

grep -c 'border:2px solid var(--accent)' docs/index.html returns 0 inside the Overview tab region (the bordered accent box is deleted). Other uses of border:2px solid var(--accent) elsewhere in the document are acceptable if they exist.
grep -c '#p3-failures' docs/index.html is unchanged or +1 vs. baseline (the hero sc-sublabel link is retained; the aside adds a second reference to the same anchor in the arithmetic paragraph).
grep -c '#part6' docs/index.html is unchanged (the Section 6.6 link moved from the bordered box into the aside; total references unchanged).

grep -c '#timestamp-error' docs/index.html increases by exactly 1 vs. baseline (the aside introduces a new cross-reference to the Timestamp Error tab; this is the only new internal anchor this EXP adds).
DevTools Computed panel on .accuracy-math-aside reports font-size between 0.88rem and 0.94rem at desktop width (the .92rem rule resolves correctly) and color resolving from var(--ink-2).
Mobile rendering at 719px width: .ama-grid collapses to a single column (grid-template-columns: 1fr) — verify with Playwright screenshot test or devtools responsive mode.
TOC generator output (Overview section entries) is byte-identical before and after this EXP integrates — the aside's ama-kicker is styled but is a <div> element, so it must not appear in the TOC.
Accessibility check: aria-labelledby on the <aside> correctly points to the ama-kicker's id; ama-kicker is programmatically reachable as the aside's accessible name.

WCAG AA contrast ratio for .92rem var(--ink-2) (EXP-202: dark = #c7c9d9) over page background (#1a1a2e) measures >= 4.5:1 at the dark theme default. A color-pair contrast test is added to test.js (see below).

The aside renders inline prose in --serif after EXP-207 lands, kicker in --sans, <code> spans in --mono; before EXP-207 lands, all text renders in the system-sans fallback and the EXP still passes all above criteria.

test.js passes with three new assertions: (a) docs/index.html contains exactly one <aside class="accuracy-math-aside"> element; (b) the Overview tab region contains no <div> with inline style starting 'border:2px solid var(--accent)'; (c) the hero sc-sublabel for 'Actual Failures' does NOT contain the substring 'Honest accuracy:'.
Build reproducibility: fresh clone + node build.js html produces byte-identical docs/index.html (up to the embedded date line) — NC6 honored.

Visual regression check: the Overview skim-reader sees thesis + stance statement + verdict bar chart BEFORE the accuracy-math aside (the aside is post-stat-card, post-breakdown, pre-Downloads). Confirm with Playwright screenshot at 1080p.

CC2 one-scroll reachability: on a 1080p viewport, scrolling from the top of the Overview reaches the aside's first arithmetic line within a single viewport-height scroll. If the bar chart (EXP-209) pushes the aside out of the first scroll, the aside's first arithmetic line must still be reachable within two scrolls — measure.

anticipated_objections.objection: Moving the headline-is-hardcoded critique from a bordered accent box to a muted aside weakens a load-bearing argument. The Timestamp Error tab's strongest cross-reference is the hardcoded-number pattern; this EXP buries that pattern.
anticipated_objections.response: The aside's source-code critique paragraph preserves every sentence of the original bordered box verbatim except the one-sentence editorial synthesis 'manually entered with a self-serving denominator' (content-preservation-audit documents every preserved clause). The cross-reference to the Timestamp Error tab is actually STRENGTHENED by this EXP — the aside adds a new explicit anchor to #timestamp-error tying the hardcoded-count pattern to the status_history.json analysis, which was only implicit in the bordered box. The demotion is visual pitch, not argumentative force. For a reader actually investigating the hardcoded-number critique, the aside's structure (two columns, explicit cross-reference, compact typography) is easier to scan than the bordered box was. For a skim-reader, the aside is appropriately subordinate to the thesis and stance statement — which is the brief's explicit goal.

anticipated_objections.objection: Two columns at ≥720px may feel cramped on mid-width viewports (e.g., tablet portrait 768–1024px). The arithmetic column's long paragraph and the source-code column's long paragraph don't necessarily want to sit side-by-side at 400px column width.
anticipated_objections.response: Acceptable tradeoff. Two columns at 768px gives each column ~340px usable width, which fits ~50 characters per line — tight but readable for metadata-level prose at 0.92rem. The alternative (single column at all widths) makes the aside nearly as tall as the deleted bordered accent box, recouping the pitch reduction with a vertical-space increase. If human review prefers a three-breakpoint approach (stack below 720px, single-column 720–960px, two-column ≥960px), the CSS change is one media query — flagged in notes_for_human_review_group. The current breakpoint (720px stack ↔ desktop grid) matches EXP-213 / EXP-A8 Item 3 mobile breakpoint guidance and is the simplest coherent choice.

anticipated_objections.objection: The aside's kicker 'Accuracy math — detail for the calibrated reader' editorializes about the audience. 'Calibrated reader' may feel condescending, and kickers should be descriptive not evaluative.
anticipated_objections.response: Flagged for human review. The alternative kickers considered were: 'Accuracy math — supporting detail' (purely descriptive, slightly flat), 'Accuracy math — the full denominator' (descriptive, promotes denominator framing), 'Honest accuracy — how the math works' (promotes our 'honest' framing into the kicker itself, which the brief's restraint direction argues against). 'Calibrated reader' was chosen because it explicitly signals to skim-readers that they can skip this surface without loss — which IS the brief's goal for this demotion (skim-readers should remember the thesis, not the percentage). If the human group prefers a purely descriptive kicker, 'Accuracy math — supporting detail' is the analyst's second-choice default. Two words, one-line change.

anticipated_objections.objection: Deleting the one-sentence synthesis 'The headline number is manually entered with a self-serving denominator' drops the word 'self-serving', which was the sharpest editorial claim in the bordered box. Demotion is one thing; de-editorializing is another.
anticipated_objections.response: Correct observation, deliberate tradeoff. The brief's restraint direction explicitly argues that pre-loading verdicts in chrome weakens credibility, and 'self-serving' is an editorial verdict inside chrome-pitch prose. The claim 'the denominator excludes unresolved open problems and below-detection-threshold entries' IS preserved — that factual substrate is what readers can use to decide for themselves whether the denominator is self-serving. The reader who concludes 'self-serving' from the preserved facts reaches the conclusion under their own power, which is a stronger rhetorical result than being told. If the human group disagrees, the phrase can be re-inserted in the aside's second paragraph final clause — two-word addition — and the curmudgeon can re-review. Flagged in notes_for_human_review_group.

anticipated_objections.objection: What about print pathway? If a reader prints the review, does the aside's low-pitch treatment (0.92rem, muted ink) still read on paper?
anticipated_objections.response: Print theme (@media print) already overrides --ink-2 and --ink-3 with darker variants for paper contrast (per EXP-202's print root). The aside inherits those overrides automatically — no per-print CSS needed. Acceptance criterion includes a print-preview visual regression check (run node build.js pdf and confirm the aside renders with readable contrast). The border-left 3px var(--rule) prints as a light grey rule, the ama-grid collapses to single column in print (consistent with print's narrow column width), and the kicker renders as a small-caps heading in the print serif stack. No print-specific failures anticipated.

anticipated_objections.objection: The aside defensively renders when accuracyVariantList is empty (zero internal data sources). The sentence 'Internal queries return — depending on which data source...' is ungrammatical with an empty variant list.
anticipated_objections.response:
Legitimate defensive-rendering concern. The current generate-html.js already sets accuracyVariantList = '' when variantSources is empty (line 1087–1089 of generate-html.js). The ama-variant paragraph becomes: 'Internal queries return depending on which data source and counting method is used' — a double space and a grammatical hiccup. Fix: wrap the ama-variant paragraph in a conditional: ${accuracyVariantList ? `

Internal queries return ${accuracyVariantList} depending on...

` : ''}. This adds four characters of template-literal ternary and prevents the grammar failure. Spec updated — conditional wrapping is in change_3's new_aside_html as a TODO. Follow-up: acceptance criterion test.js assertion (d) is added: when variantSources is empty, the aside contains no ama-variant paragraph and contains no ungrammatical whitespace (substring 'Internal queries return ' with double-space does not appear). The aside still renders grammatically with only the main arithmetic paragraph, the source-code paragraph, and the ama-cross paragraph. IMPLEMENTATION NOTE: the new_aside_html above shows the unconditional form for clarity; the decider should wrap ama-variant in the ternary when integrating.

anticipated_objections.objection: Is <aside> the right semantic element, or should this be <section> with an explicit heading? The WAI-ARIA practice for <aside> is 'tangentially related content'; the accuracy math is NOT tangentially related — it's a direct elaboration of the stat card.
anticipated_objections.response: Judgment call, flagged. The WAI-ARIA definition of <aside> is indeed 'tangentially related to the main content', and a strict reading says the accuracy-math aside is supporting, not tangential — <section aria-labelledby='accuracy-math-aside-heading'> with the kicker promoted to a heading (h3 or styled div with role='heading') would be technically stricter. The analyst defaulted to <aside> because: (a) the visual pitch (border-left, smaller font, reduced prominence) is exactly the role <aside> typically serves in document layout; (b) many screen readers announce 'complementary' for <aside>, which is a signal of 'here's a sidebar, you can skip or dive in', matching the brief's 'skim-reader should skip' goal; (c) using <section> would require a real h3 heading which WOULD enter the TOC. If human review prefers <section>, the edit is <aside → section> + <div class='ama-kicker'> → <h3 class='ama-kicker'> with a TOC-exclusion mechanism, which is more moving parts. Analyst's recommendation: keep <aside> until EXP-213 / Item 3 accessibility audit (Phase A) makes a comprehensive decision across all supporting-content elements.

counter_considerations_the_analyst_must_.title: 'Kill Shots' is not chrome
counter_considerations_the_analyst_must_.addressed_how: Not touched by this EXP. Kill Shots tab label, button styling, kill-shot heading badges — all unchanged. EXP-210 / EXP-A5 owns the red-palette pass; this EXP is strictly the accuracy-math demotion.

counter_considerations_the_analyst_must_.title: 4+4 failure accounting is calibration content
counter_considerations_the_analyst_must_.addressed_how: Explicitly preserved. The hero 4+4 Actual Failures sc-card keeps its sc-number (${failures.dome_claimed_failures} + ${silentFailures}), its sc-label ('Actual Failures'), and the He-admits/We-found narrative in its sublabel. Only the 'Honest accuracy: X, not Y' arithmetic clause is stripped — because that arithmetic moves to the aside. The Details link (#p3-failures) is kept on the hero card AND added to the aside. One-scroll reachability (CC2 acceptance) is verified in the acceptance_criteria section — on 1080p the aside sits within 1–2 viewport scrolls of the hero card.

counter_considerations_the_analyst_must_.title: Latest Findings is evidence, not ticker chrome
counter_considerations_the_analyst_must_.addressed_how: Not touched by this EXP. EXP-A7 owns Latest Findings visual treatment.

counter_considerations_the_analyst_must_.title: Dark theme is differentiator, not oversight
counter_considerations_the_analyst_must_.addressed_how: The .accuracy-math-aside CSS uses --ink-2, --ink-3, and --rule tokens, all of which have dark-theme values defined by EXP-202. Background is transparent (inherits page bg = #1a1a2e in dark), border-left is var(--rule) = dark-mode low-contrast rule. Designed for dark-theme primary; print inherits EXP-202's print-root overrides automatically. No paper-theme aesthetic imported.

counter_considerations_the_analyst_must_.title: Hero thesis candidate is a CANDIDATE
counter_considerations_the_analyst_must_.addressed_how: Not touched by this EXP. Hero thesis sentence is EXP-A3 scope.

counter_considerations_the_analyst_must_.title: Verdict distribution chart must route through generate-html.js computed counts
counter_considerations_the_analyst_must_.addressed_how: The aside routes through generate-html.js computed context variables (${counts.total}, ${failures.dome_claimed_failures}, ${silentFailures}, ${honestAccuracy}, ${failures.dome_claimed_accuracy}, ${accuracyVariantList}). No hardcoded number introduced anywhere. NC5 honored — every numeric claim in the aside is computed at build time.

counter_considerations_the_analyst_must_.title: Stance statement is operator-authored
counter_considerations_the_analyst_must_.addressed_how: Not touched by this EXP. EXP-A3 owns the stance statement.

non_negotiable_constraints_preserved.title: Dark theme stays
non_negotiable_constraints_preserved.how: All tokens consumed (--ink-2, --ink-3, --rule, --sans, --mono) have dark-theme overrides defined in EXP-202. Aside readability verified for dark primary theme in acceptance_criteria WCAG AA check.

non_negotiable_constraints_preserved.title: Tabs stay
non_negotiable_constraints_preserved.how: No IA change. The aside lives inside the Overview tab, does not introduce or remove tabs, does not change tab labels or button styling.

non_negotiable_constraints_preserved.title: 'Kill Shots' term stays
non_negotiable_constraints_preserved.how: No reference to Kill Shots touched by this EXP. The Kill Shots tab remains as-is; this EXP works the Overview tab only.

non_negotiable_constraints_preserved.title: Latest Findings stays on overview
non_negotiable_constraints_preserved.how: Latest Findings breaking-news block is not touched by this EXP. Its position (after the hero scorecard, before the sc-breakdown) relative to the new aside (after sc-breakdown, before Downloads) is explicitly preserved — the aside does NOT move Latest Findings off the Overview.

non_negotiable_constraints_preserved.title: Computed counts rule
non_negotiable_constraints_preserved.how: Every numeric value in the aside is derived from generate-html.js context variables. No hardcoded percentages, counts, or denominators. test.js assertion (new) greps for hardcoded-looking percentages inside .accuracy-math-aside and fails the build if any are found outside the expected template substitutions.

non_negotiable_constraints_preserved.title: Build reproducibility
non_negotiable_constraints_preserved.how: All changes are static template literal edits and CSS additions. Fresh clone + node build.js html produces byte-identical docs/index.html. Acceptance criterion required.

non_negotiable_constraints_preserved.title: Tests green
non_negotiable_constraints_preserved.how: Three new assertions added to test.js (listed in acceptance_criteria). Existing assertions unchanged. Baseline test count rises by 3; all pass.

preserved_load_bearing_content: Hero scorecard 4+4 Actual Failures sc-card: sc-number (the computed ${failures.dome_claimed_failures} + ${silentFailures}), sc-label ('Actual Failures'), He-admits/We-found narrative sentence, and the #p3-failures Details link — all retained verbatim
Honest accuracy percentage (${honestAccuracy}) — relocated to aside but preserved; still computed from ${counts.total}, ${failures.dome_claimed_failures}, ${silentFailures}
Accuracy variant list (${accuracyVariantList}) — relocated from bordered box to aside's ama-variant paragraph, preserved verbatim

Source-code critique clauses: 'not computed by any script', 'static string in the HTML source', '.score-number CSS class', 'No Python script, no JavaScript function, and no API endpoint produces this number', 'arithmetic is stated on the wins page, but no script validates the count' — all verbatim in aside
Internal anchor #part6 (Section 6.6 full source-code analysis) — retained in aside's source-code paragraph
Internal anchor #p3-failures — retained in hero sc-card (Details link) and ALSO added to aside's arithmetic paragraph
Silent Failures static-template sc-card in sections.json part1.html — not touched by this EXP; {{HONEST_ACCURACY}} token there is unchanged

docs/index.html build pipeline — the generate-html.js computed context variables (accuracyVariantList, accuracyVariantDetail, honestAccuracy) continue to be computed exactly as they are today; only their DOM placement changes
out_of_scope_for_this_exp: Red-palette neutralization on the hero 4+4 sc-card border-left (#c45050 → var(--rule)) — EXP-210 / EXP-A5 owns that pass. This EXP's strip-the-arithmetic edit is independent of the chroma edit and the two can ship in either order.
Silent Failures static template in sections.json part1.html — that surface is pre-hero static and outside this EXP's scope; a follow-up issue can harmonize it if integrity flags the duplication.
Typography application (EXP-A2 / EXP-207) — this EXP consumes --sans / --mono tokens but does not apply typography to other Overview surfaces.
Accessibility glyph+label pairing on verdict pills (EXP-A8) — the aside's aria-labelledby is included but a comprehensive a11y audit is Phase A8 scope.

Moving the Timestamp Error cross-reference into a dedicated 'related reading' box — the aside's ama-cross paragraph does it inline; a more ambitious information-architecture pass (e.g., universal related-reading footer per tab) is Phase B scope.
Redesigning the hero scorecard layout itself (grid-template-columns, card proportions) — not touched. Only the third card's sublabel content is edited.
Re-rendering the pie-chart SVG with var() fills — EXP-210 notes_for_human_review_group flags this as Phase B.
Removing the second 'Honest accuracy: X, not Y' occurrence from the bordered box (already deleted by this EXP — no residual occurrence).
notes_for_human_review_group: KICKER PHRASING: 'Accuracy math — detail for the calibrated reader' editorializes about the audience ('calibrated' = evaluative). Analyst's second-choice default: 'Accuracy math — supporting detail' (purely descriptive). Group preference welcome; two-word change.

DROPPED SENTENCE: The one-sentence editorial synthesis 'The headline number is manually entered with a self-serving denominator' is removed. Factual substrate ('denominator excludes unresolved open problems and below-detection-threshold entries') is preserved. Analyst judges the removal consistent with the brief's restraint direction, but group may prefer to re-insert the phrase. Two-word addition to restore if desired.

SEMANTIC ELEMENT: <aside> vs. <section aria-labelledby>. Analyst chose <aside> because the visual role matches complementary/skippable and it avoids a new TOC entry. Strict reading of WAI-ARIA would prefer <section> for 'directly related supporting detail'. Flag for group decision or defer to EXP-A8 accessibility audit.

BREAKPOINT: Two-column grid at ≥720px, stack below. Tablet portrait (768–1024px) has tight columns. Alternative: three-breakpoint (stack ≤720px, single-column 720–960px, two-column ≥960px). Analyst's default is two-breakpoint for simplicity; group preference welcome.

EXP-A1 ORDERING: This EXP consumes tokens from EXP-202 (--ink-2, --ink-3, --rule, --sans, --mono). EXP-202 is in the Phase A1 batch. If A1 integrates as a batch including EXP-202 before this A2 EXP ships, no issue. If A2 somehow integrates first, the aside still renders with system-sans fallback and browser-default grey ink — usable but unpolished. Recommend: block A2 integration on A1 integration at minimum.

NEW CROSS-REFERENCE: The aside introduces an anchor to #timestamp-error that did not exist in the old bordered box. This strengthens the content relationship between the hardcoded-number critique and the Timestamp Error tab's status_history.json analysis. Flagged because any new internal anchor is a content decision, however small. The linked surface (#timestamp-error) exists in the current build and is stable.

DEFENSIVE RENDERING: The ama-variant paragraph is conditionally wrapped in ${accuracyVariantList ? ... : ''} so an empty variant list produces zero ungrammatical artifacts. Implementation noted in anticipated_objections #6; decider should wrap during integration.

PARALLEL TO EXP-210: EXP-210 neutralizes the hero 4+4 sc-card border-left chroma (#c45050 → var(--rule)). This EXP strips the arithmetic from that same sc-card's sublabel. The two edits are independent and the order does not matter — integration can batch them without coordination.

NAMED APPROVAL GATE: Per HNOTE-DESIGN-REFRESH-EXP-211-REDRAFT-001, the approval HNOTE id for this EXP's Phase A2 integration is 'HNOTE-DESIGN-REFRESH-PHASE-A2-INTEGRATION-APPROVED-NNN' (distinct from the A1 batch HNOTE). Operator indicated A2 will ship separately after A1 lands.

ISS-1351 CLOSURE: This redraft is the resolution for ISS-1351. The issue-proposal companion to this file (proposal-EXP-211-redesign-draft-ready.json) should include a close-on-fix annotation for ISS-1351 so the decider closes the issue when this EXP ships.

CURMUDGEON REVIEW: Per HNOTE-DESIGN-REFRESH-EXP-211-REDRAFT-001, this EXP should route to curmudgeon for adversarial review. If the revised truth-vs-standards rubric (DIRECTIVE-20260422-003) has landed by review time, the curmudgeon applies it; otherwise the review proceeds under the existing rubric with a note that re-review may be needed if DIRECTIVE-20260422-003 lands later and materially changes standards.

COLLISION ROOT CAUSE: The workspace EXP-211 file was overwritten on 2026-04-22 by WIN-038 eigenvalue content (EXP-211-WIN038-coupled-eigenvalue.json and EXP-211.json both contain the WIN-038 patch). Output filename for this redraft is intentionally 'EXP-211-redesign.json' to avoid a second collision. The decider and tracker should treat this redesign file as the authoritative Phase A2 content for EXP-211; the collided EXP-211.json (WIN-038 content) continues to carry the WIN-038 patch under its own item_id wrapper inside — if the decider wants to rename that file to match the WIN-038-coupled-eigenvalue filename for clarity, that is a trivial rename (no content change).

newsworthiness_caveat: Accuracy-math demotion on its own is not newsworthy — no verdict change, no claim change, no new evidence. The full Phase A landing (A1 batch + A2 EXP-211-redesign integrated) may be newsworthy as a credibility-through-restraint design refresh. Reserve the headline for a Phase A completion EXP, not EXP-211.

integration_hold.blocked_until: operator posts HNOTE-DESIGN-REFRESH-PHASE-A2-INTEGRATION-APPROVED-NNN in monitor/decisions/human-notes.json with approved_exp_ids containing 'EXP-211' AND status 'pending' at decider read time
unlock_checklist: (a) approval HNOTE exists with id matching 'HNOTE-DESIGN-REFRESH-PHASE-A2-INTEGRATION-APPROVED-NNN'
(c) author='operator', status='pending' at decider read time
(d) if approval HNOTE references a curmudgeon review ID, that review is not superseded
(e) Phase A1 (EXP-202) has integrated OR the approval HNOTE explicitly waives the A1-ordering dependency
integration_hold.reason: HNOTE-DESIGN-REFRESH-PHASE-A-001 hard-hold: every Phase A EXP carries DRAFT_AWAITING_HUMAN_GROUP_REVIEW until a named approval HNOTE lands referencing the specific EXP ID. Per HNOTE-DESIGN-REFRESH-EXP-211-REDRAFT-001, A2 integration uses a distinct HNOTE from the A1 batch.

completion_signal.action: Signal to the decider: 'Redraft complete. Do NOT integrate until HNOTE-DESIGN-REFRESH-PHASE-A2-INTEGRATION-APPROVED-NNN lands with approved_exp_ids containing EXP-211 AND Phase A1 (EXP-202 tokens minimum) has integrated.' Include close-on-fix annotation for ISS-1351.

close_on_fix.resolution: EXP-211 redrafted as monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-211-redesign.json per HNOTE-DESIGN-REFRESH-EXP-211-REDRAFT-001. Phase A2 ready for operator approval HNOTE.

completion_signal.curmudgeon_request_notes: Phase A2 design EXP — adversarial review before operator-group approval. Probes: (a) does the aside pitch actually demote below hero scorecard? Measure font-size, border weight, and vertical area ratios. (b) CC2 preserved? Screenshot-test one-scroll reachability at 1080p; measure distance from hero card to aside arithmetic line. (c) content-preservation — diff the aside prose against the removed bordered-box prose and the stripped sublabel clause; confirm verbatim preservation of all source-code-critique clauses. (d) NC5 computed-counts rule — grep aside HTML for any hardcoded numbers or percentages outside template substitutions. (e) WCAG AA contrast for .92rem var(--ink-2) over #1a1a2e dark-theme page bg — measure. (f) defensive rendering for empty accuracyVariantList — confirm the ternary wrap in change_3 is implemented and renders grammatically. (g) semantic element: <aside> vs. <section aria-labelledby> — weigh strict WAI-ARIA reading vs. visual-role match. (h) kicker phrasing: editorializes? group preference? (i) dropped 'self-serving denominator' sentence — does the factual substrate carry the claim adequately? (j) breakpoint at 720px — does tablet portrait (768–1024) render readably? (k) aside position vs. Latest Findings — confirm aside sits after sc-breakdown and before Downloads, NOT between hero and breaking-news. (l) assertions testability — do the three new test.js assertions correctly detect the intended invariants (hero sublabel lacks 'Honest accuracy:', bordered accent box deleted, single aside element present)? Apply DIRECTIVE-20260422-003 curmudgeon truth-vs-standards rubric if landed by review time; otherwise note pending status.

tldr_evidence: Phase A2 redraft of EXP-211 per operator's HNOTE-DESIGN-REFRESH-EXP-211-REDRAFT-001. Consolidates the two Overview accuracy-math callouts (hero scorecard Actual Failures sublabel arithmetic clause + bordered accent box about headline being hardcoded) into a single <aside class="accuracy-math-aside"> placed after the sc-breakdown block and before Downloads. The aside uses two-column grid at ≥720px (arithmetic + source-code critique side-by-side), stacks below, and renders at 0.92rem in --ink-2 with a 3px var(--rule) left border — lower pitch than the bordered accent box it replaces. All arithmetic, source-code critique clauses, and internal anchors (#p3-failures, #part6) are preserved; #timestamp-error is newly added as a cross-reference. Only the one editorial synthesis sentence ('self-serving denominator') is dropped — factual substrate preserved.
tldr_verdict: DRAFT — Phase A2 accuracy-math demotion only, no verdict change, no claim change. Hero 4+4 Actual Failures sc-card keeps its count, label, He-admits/We-found narrative, and Details link; only the 'Honest accuracy: X, not Y' clause is stripped and moves to the aside. Bordered accent box is deleted wholesale and its content (verbatim) moves to the aside's second column. Integration blocked until operator posts HNOTE-DESIGN-REFRESH-PHASE-A2-INTEGRATION-APPROVED-NNN naming EXP-211 AND EXP-202 tokens have integrated in Phase A1.
revision_notes_2: Re-inserted sentence 'The headline number is manually entered with a self-serving denominator.' per HNOTE-EXP-211-REDESIGN-REINSERT-HEADLINE-SENTENCE-001 (operator, 2026-04-23T03:20Z). Sentence placed in ama-col-source first paragraph, before the Section 6.6 link. Original analyst framing (HNOTE drop as deliberate tradeoff) overruled by operator direction: 'design cannot eat truth'. ISS-1437 (curmudgeon c1 H1 finding of the dropped sentence) closed. Curmudgeon queue item pushed for EXP-211-redesign to verify sentence survived round-trip.
